hypothesis: The best model so far uses tweedie_variance_power=1.5 (WRMSSE=0.94488). Hypothesis: the M5 data has many zero sales and a heavy right tail, so a lower Tweedie power (1.1) which is closer to Poisson may better fit the distribution by placing more weight on zero/near-zero counts. All other settings match best run (exp_0033): num_leaves=256, max_depth=8, learning_rate=0.04, n_estimators=800, early_stopping_rounds=50.
